brand.taglineShort: Clear how-to answers for every phone you own.

methodology: Every how-to is written against a physical device or the exact OS version named in the title — we re-check steps after major iOS and One UI updates.
Screenshots and menu paths use the on-device labels readers will see, not generic Android/iOS jargon.
Battery and troubleshooting guides list what we ruled out before recommending a reset or repair.
Review articles disclose purchase source, test duration, and carrier/SIM context.
Affiliate links are limited to cases, chargers, and storage tools we actually use in testing.
Reader corrections on wrong menu paths are fixed within 72 hours with a dated changelog line.

newsletter.title: The PhoneHowTo weekly
newsletter.subtitle: Practical picks and straight answers from PhoneHowTo, once a week. No spam.

defaultMetaDescription: Model-specific how-tos for iPhone, Galaxy, Pixel, and Motorola. Screenshots, backups, battery drain, storage full, frozen screen, transfer to a new phone — no fluff, no 12-minute intro.

homepage.h1: The phone question you Googled at midnight — answered in plain steps.
homepage.subhead: Model-specific how-tos for iPhone, Galaxy, Pixel, and Motorola. Screenshots, backups, battery drain, storage full, frozen screen, transfer to a new phone — no fluff, no 12-minute intro.
homepage.trustLine: We buy or borrow phones at retail. Affiliate links only on accessories we would use ourselves.

about.headline: Why PhoneHowTo exists
about.body: Official support pages bury the answer under menus. YouTube videos take eight minutes to show one setting. PhoneHowTo is the opposite: one search query, one page, numbered steps with the exact menu names for your model. We cover the high-volume how-tos first — then expand into honest short reviews when a phone is worth calling out.
